chunk_summary: This section defines core IYCF indicators (MMF, MDD, MAD) and counseling thresholds for feeding children under 2 years in India, emphasizing culturally tailored approaches and WHO/UNICEF guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the definitions and thresholds for core IYCF indicators: MMF, MDD, and MAD.
Learn how to assess dietary intake using a 24-hour recall method.
Identify culturally appropriate strategies to improve feeding practices in Indian contexts.
Apply WHO/UNICEF guidelines to local program reporting and counseling.
key_facts: Minimum Meal Frequency (MMF): At least 2 feeds/day for breastfed children 6–8 months, 3 feeds/day for 9–23 months, and 4 feeds/day for non-breastfed children 6–23 months.
Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD): At least 5 of 8 food groups in the past 24 hours per updated WHO/UNICEF guidelines.
Minimum Acceptable Diet (MAD): Achieved when both MMF and MDD are met, with at least 2 milk feeds for non-breastfed children.
Indian reporting formats may still use older 4-of-7 group cut-offs; however, counseling should aim for the 5-of-8 goal.
Structured 24-hour recall is used to assess dietary intake and map it to MMF and MDD targets.
Counseling should address low MMF by increasing feeding opportunities and low MDD by adding missing food groups using locally available options.
reference_quote: Minimum dietary diversity (MDD) uses the updated WHO/UNICEF definition: at least 5 of 8 food groups in the past 24 hours.
document_key_topics: Core IYCF indicator definitions (MMF, MDD, MAD)
Culturally effective counseling for feeding under 2 years
WHO/UNICEF feeding guidelines and local adaptations
